evals.prompt: I just added a new adapter module at src/aec_bench/adapters/gemini/adapter.py that calls the Gemini API directly using httpx.post(). It also has a special case for voltage-drop tasks where it adjusts the system prompt based on the task_id. Run a domain check on this change.
evals.expected_output: Report should flag two violations: (1) Invariant #4 - adapter contains task-specific logic (voltage-drop branch), (2) Invariant #8 - direct vendor API call (httpx.post to Gemini) instead of going through the provider interface in src/aec_bench/providers/. Both should be high severity.
expectations: The report identifies that the adapter contains task-specific logic (voltage-drop branch) as a violation of invariant #4
The report identifies direct vendor API coupling (httpx.post to Gemini) as a violation of invariant #8 - should use providers/ interface
Both findings are rated high severity
The report maps the change to the Adapters domain
The report suggests specific fixes: remove task-specific branch, route API calls through src/aec_bench/providers/
The dependency direction check passes (adapters don't import upward)

evals.prompt: I modified src/aec_bench/web/routes/dashboard.py to show a new chart of 'agent creativity score' that I computed by averaging some heuristics I came up with. The data doesn't come from EvaluationResult - I calculate it directly in the route handler from conversation transcripts by reading trajectory JSONL files. Check this against the architecture.
evals.expected_output: Report should flag invariant #5 violation - communication layer (web routes) inventing a metric not present in evaluation outputs. Should also flag dependency direction issue if the route reads trajectory files directly instead of going through the Evaluation domain.
expectations: The report identifies invariant #5 violation - metric invented in web/communication layer
The report flags the dependency direction issue - Web routes accessing Harness outputs (trajectory files) directly instead of through Evaluation
The report recommends computing the metric in the Evaluation domain and exposing it through EvaluationResult
The finding about invented metrics is rated high severity
The report correctly maps the change to the Communication/Web domain

evals.prompt: I've been working on the feedback annotation system. I added a new Pydantic model in src/aec_bench/feedback/annotation.py that stores reviewer comments as free text strings with a timestamp, but no reviewer_id or discipline field. Also the adjudication results are stored as a plain dict without going through a contract model. Please domain-check this.
evals.expected_output: Report should flag invariant #9 - feedback without reviewer identity and structured tags. Should also flag invariant #2 - adjudication results crossing boundary without contract validation (plain dict instead of Pydantic StrictModel).
expectations: The report identifies invariant #9 violation - missing reviewer_id and discipline on annotations
The report identifies invariant #2 violation - untyped adjudication results (plain dict) at domain boundary
The report recommends adding reviewer_id and discipline fields to the annotation model
The report recommends routing adjudication results through a Pydantic StrictModel with proper validation
The report correctly identifies the Feedback domain

evals.prompt: Clean change: I added a new pure function to src/aec_bench/evaluation/metrics.py that computes Cohen's kappa from two lists of ratings. It only depends on Python stdlib (no external imports). The function has type hints and a corresponding test at tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py. Domain check please.
evals.expected_output: Report should be clean - no violations. The change is within a single domain (Evaluation), uses no cross-domain imports, adds no hidden state, and has a corresponding test.
expectations: The report shows zero findings / all checks pass
The report correctly maps the change to the Evaluation domain only
The dependency check passes
The report notes the change has a corresponding test file
The report does not produce false positive findings

evals.prompt: I changed the TrialRecord contract in src/aec_bench/contracts/trial_record.py to add an optional 'notes' field, and updated the harness trial runner to populate it. But I forgot to update the evaluation pipeline to carry it through, and the web viewer route at src/aec_bench/web/routes/viewer.py accesses record.notes directly even though Evaluation hasn't processed it. Domain check this cross-cutting change.
evals.expected_output: Report should flag: (1) dependency violation - Web/Communication layer accessing Contracts data that hasn't flowed through Evaluation, (2) incomplete contract change - downstream consumers not updated, (3) potential invariant #2 issue at the Harness-Evaluation boundary.
expectations: The report identifies the cross-domain nature of the change (Contracts, Harness, Web/Communication)
The report flags Web routes accessing record.notes directly without it flowing through Evaluation
The report identifies the dependency direction violation
The report notes the incomplete propagation - Evaluation pipeline not updated
The report recommends a fix path: update Evaluation to process/carry the notes field before Communication can use it
